section_name: Distribution of Fee for Permit for Excess Weight
chapter_title: Gen. Provisions Relating to Vehicle Size & Weight
text:
(a) The comptroller shall send $50 of each base fee collected under Section 623.011 (Permit for Excess Axle or Gross Weight) for an excess weight permit to the counties of the state, with each county receiving an amount determined according to the ratio of the total number of miles of county roads maintained by the county to the total number of miles of county roads maintained by all of the counties of this state. The comptroller shall deposit $40 of each base fee, plus each fee collected under Section 623.0112 (Additional Administrative Fee) , to the credit of the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles fund. Money deposited to the credit of that fund under this subsection may be appropriated only to the department to administer this section and Sections 623.011 (Permit for Excess Axle or Gross Weight) , 623.0111 (Additional Fee for Operation of Vehicle Under Permit) , and 623.0112 (Additional Administrative Fee) .

(b) The comptroller shall send the amount due each county under Subsection (a) to the county treasurer or officer performing the function of that office at least twice each fiscal year.

(c) The comptroller shall send each fee collected under Section 623.0111 (Additional Fee for Operation of Vehicle Under Permit) for an excess weight permit to the counties designated on the application for the permit, with each county shown on the application receiving an amount determined according to the ratio of the total number of miles of county roads maintained by the county to the total number of miles of county roads maintained by all of the counties designated on the application.

(d) The county treasurer or officer shall deposit amounts received under this section to the credit of the county road and bridge fund. Money deposited to the credit of that fund under this subsection may be used only for a purpose authorized by Section 256.001 (Use of County Road and Bridge Fund) (a).
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 621. Gen. Provisions Relating to Vehicle Size & Weight
##### § 621.353 — Distribution of Fee for Permit for Excess Weight

(a) The comptroller shall send $50 of each base fee collected under Section 623.011 (Permit for Excess Axle or Gross Weight) for an excess weight permit to the counties of the state, with each county receiving an amount determined according to the ratio of the total number of miles of county roads maintained by the county to the total number of miles of county roads maintained by all of the counties of this state. The comptroller shall deposit $40 of each base fee, plus each fee collected under Section 623.0112 (Additional Administrative Fee) , to the credit of the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles fund. Money deposited to the credit of that fund under this subsection may be appropriated only to the department to administer this section and Sections 623.011 (Permit for Excess Axle or Gross Weight) , 623.0111 (Additional Fee for Operation of Vehicle Under Permit) , and 623.0112 (Additional Administrative Fee) .

(b) The comptroller shall send the amount due each county under Subsection (a) to the county treasurer or officer performing the function of that office at least twice each fiscal year.

(c) The comptroller shall send each fee collected under Section 623.0111 (Additional Fee for Operation of Vehicle Under Permit) for an excess weight permit to the counties designated on the application for the permit, with each county shown on the application receiving an amount determined according to the ratio of the total number of miles of county roads maintained by the county to the total number of miles of county roads maintained by all of the counties designated on the application.

(d) The county treasurer or officer shall deposit amounts received under this section to the credit of the county road and bridge fund. Money deposited to the credit of that fund under this subsection may be used only for a purpose authorized by Section 256.001 (Use of County Road and Bridge Fund) (a).

*Acts 2013, 83rd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1287 (H.B.2202), Sec. 49, eff. September 1, 2013.*

The comptroller shall deposit $40 of each base fee, plus each fee collected under Section 623.0112 (Additional Administrative Fee) , to the credit of the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles fund. Money deposited to the credit of that fund under this subsection may be appropriated only to the department to administer this section and Sections 623.011 (Permit for Excess Axle or Gross Weight) , 623.0111 (Additional Fee for Operation of Vehicle Under Permit) , and 623.0112 (Additional Administrative Fee) .

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: The county treasurer or officer shall deposit amounts received under this section to the credit of the county road and bridge fund. Money deposited to the credit of that fund under this subsection may be used only for a purpose authorized by Section 256.001 (Use of County Road and Bridge Fund) (a).
